title: Maas Energy Works Commissions Renewable Natural Gas (RNG) Interconnection in Collaboration with Pacific Gas and Electric Company
content: Maas Energy Works announced the commissioning of a new renewable natural gas interconnection at the Couco Creek Dairy in Turlock, California, linking the facility to the Pacific Gas and Electric Company natural gas system and supporting the expansion of dairyâ€‘derived biomethane across the region. The facility highlights advancements in biomethane and RNG technology by bringing together two supply pathways: onsite RNG generated from livestock waste that is converted into pipelineâ€‘grade fuel, and additional RNG delivered from offsite production facilities through a dedicated offloading and decompression setup. This configuration allows the site to function as a hub that blends both onsite production and truckedâ€‘in gas, enabling dairies without direct pipeline access to contribute to the renewable gas supply and increasing overall system efficiency. By capturing methane that would otherwise enter the atmosphere and upgrading it into usable fuel, the project reinforces the role of dairyâ€‘based RNG in reducing emissions while supporting local agricultural economies. The hubâ€‘style approach is presented as a model for broader participation in Californiaâ€™s cleanâ€‘energy efforts, with the delivered gas undergoing PG&Eâ€™s required safety checks and gasâ€‘quality verification before injection into the pipeline system, reflecting a step toward expanding lowâ€‘carbon energy options for the state.
author: Analysts Account
